markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games and parent company Take-Two Interactive have locked Grand Theft Auto VI for a November 19, 2026 launch on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X/S after two prior delays, with the date now embedded in the publisher’s fiscal 2027 revenue forecast. Recent earnings reports confirm the timeline remains firm, shifting industry focus to summer marketing milestones such as a potential third trailer and pre-order campaigns. The extended runway heightens scrutiny on development polish, online features, and competitive landscape, while traders monitor any signals of further slippage or accelerated promotion that could influence which external events resolve ahead of the highly anticipated open-world title.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games and parent company Take-Two Interactive have twice officially delayed Grand Theft Auto VI, first shifting it from a 2025 window to May 26, 2026, then to November 19, 2026, explicitly to allow extra polish time. Recent earnings calls and statements have reaffirmed the November target with no signs of acceleration, anchoring trader consensus around the extended timeline and historical patterns of Rockstar prioritizing quality over speed for major releases. While an unexpected early drop remains theoretically possible given the studio’s secrecy, the consistent corporate guidance and passed May milestone make such a scenario highly improbable.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial, declared May 15 after a deadlocked jury, have reinforced trader consensus that additional prison time beyond his existing California sentence is unlikely. At 74, Weinstein’s health complications and the repeated failure of prosecutors to secure a unanimous verdict on the Jessica Mann charge have shifted sentiment toward “No Prison Time” at 74% implied probability. The 2025 split verdict in New York produced one conviction that carries potential sentencing later this year, yet appeals on both the New York and California cases continue to create uncertainty around effective time served. Historical patterns in high-profile entertainment cases involving elderly defendants further support market pricing that long additional terms face significant practical barriers. Upcoming sentencing hearings and any prosecution decision on a fourth trial remain key near-term catalysts.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes' league-best 53-22-7 regular-season record and first-round sweep have positioned them as the frontrunner in trader consensus, bolstered by home-ice advantage in their Eastern Conference Final series against the Montreal Canadiens. The Vegas Golden Knights sit close behind after sweeping the Presidents' Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche to reach the Stanley Cup Final, highlighting their depth and resilience through a demanding playoff path. The Canadiens remain a distant third following an improbable run that included Game 7 victories, though their lower regular-season standing and current series deficit limit their implied title odds. These developments in the conference finals continue to shape market positioning ahead of a potential Hurricanes-Golden Knights showdown.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Montreal Canadiens win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes' league-best 53-22-7 regular-season record and first-round sweep have positioned them as the frontrunner in trader consensus, bolstered by home-ice advantage in their Eastern Conference Final series against the Montreal Canadiens. The Vegas Golden Knights sit close behind after sweeping the Presidents' Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche to reach the Stanley Cup Final, highlighting their depth and resilience through a demanding playoff path. The Canadiens remain a distant third following an improbable run that included Game 7 victories, though their lower regular-season standing and current series deficit limit their implied title odds. These developments in the conference finals continue to shape market positioning ahead of a potential Hurricanes-Golden Knights showdown.

markets.question: Will the Oklahoma City Thunder win the 2026 NBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Oklahoma City Thunder win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus for the 2026 NBA Champion at 43.5% thanks to their league-best 64-18 regular-season record, top Western Conference standing, and established core featuring Shai Gilgeous-Alexander alongside deep roster continuity that has translated into consistent playoff success. The New York Knicks sit at 30.8% on the strength of their 53-29 mark and third-place Eastern finish, bolstered by recent series dominance and favorable bracket positioning. San Antonio Spurs hold 24.9% amid their 62-20 campaign and Victor Wembanyama-led resurgence, with momentum from a tightly contested Western Conference Finals series against the Thunder highlighting their competitive edge and recent adjustments. These implied probabilities reflect the wisdom of crowds assessing current form, conference paths, and historical playoff patterns across the three frontrunners.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France hold the narrowest edges in the 2026 World Cup winner market as European champions and recent finalists with exceptional squad depth and attacking talent. England follows closely behind on the back of consecutive major-tournament finals appearances, while Portugal, Brazil, and Argentina remain within striking distance thanks to star-driven lineups and proven knockout experience. The tight spread among the top six reflects balanced qualification results, minimal major injuries among frontrunners, and a draw that avoided early heavyweight clashes for most contenders. No single side has separated itself decisively in recent form or head-to-head trends, leaving traders pricing a genuinely open field one month before the June kickoff.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France hold the narrowest edges in the 2026 World Cup winner market as European champions and recent finalists with exceptional squad depth and attacking talent. England follows closely behind on the back of consecutive major-tournament finals appearances, while Portugal, Brazil, and Argentina remain within striking distance thanks to star-driven lineups and proven knockout experience. The tight spread among the top six reflects balanced qualification results, minimal major injuries among frontrunners, and a draw that avoided early heavyweight clashes for most contenders. No single side has separated itself decisively in recent form or head-to-head trends, leaving traders pricing a genuinely open field one month before the June kickoff.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's entrenched control as General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party and Chairman of the Central Military Commission underpins the 92.7% trader consensus against removal before the end of 2026. Recent military leadership purges and broader personnel adjustments through anti-corruption campaigns have further centralized authority without visible elite opposition or a designated successor. High-level diplomatic engagements throughout 2026, alongside the absence of confirmed public health concerns or institutional challenges, reinforce continuity. The next major leadership evaluation occurs at the 21st Party Congress scheduled for late 2027, outside the market's resolution window ending December 31, 2026. This political timeline and power consolidation explain why traders price the probability of an earlier exit as low.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination still more than two years away, traders assign Gavin Newsom the highest implied probability at 24.1 percent, reflecting his visibility as California governor and established national fundraising network. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Kamala Harris follow at 10.0 percent and 8.3 percent respectively in a fragmented field where no candidate exceeds 25 percent. Key differentiators include state executive records, congressional seniority, ideological positioning within the party, and donor access. Midterm results, primary debates, and early endorsements could consolidate support, though structural factors such as term limits and eligibility requirements continue to shape the timeline.
